summary: This notebook provides a minimal 6-cell starter template for the Google Tunix Hackathon, demonstrating how to load CSV data, initialize a Gemma causal language model via Flax, and run a basic GRPO training loop using the Tunix library. It shows how to format prompts, execute a single training step, generate text outputs in a structured <reasoning>…</reasoning> and <answer>…</answer> format, and save a demo submission. Readers should understand the foundational workflow for aligning open-weight LLMs with custom reward functions and generating competition-ready outputs. The author explicitly notes this is an educational baseline, not a competitive solution, and encourages participants to extend the reward function and tune hyperparameters.
competition_flow: Loads a CSV dataset of questions, initializes a Gemma-2B model with Flax and Tunix, runs a skeleton GRPO training step, generates structured reasoning and answer outputs via model.generate, and exports a demo submission CSV.
data_reading: Loads train.csv from the Kaggle input directory into a pandas DataFrame using pd.read_csv.
insights: Demonstrates how to integrate the Tunix library with Flax and Hugging Face Transformers for GRPO-based alignment.
Shows a minimal prompt formatting strategy to enforce structured <reasoning> and <answer> tags in model outputs.
Illustrates that a functional training loop can be bootstrapped in just a few cells using Tunix's high-level Trainer API.
